claim: MCP servers can be combined with vector databases like Supabase to build private RAG knowledge bases without relying on proprietary services like Context7
actionable: Implement custom MCP server with Crawl4AI to scrape documentation, chunk it, and store embeddings in self-hosted Supabase for private RAG without third-party dependencies
source_title: I Built the Ultimate RAG MCP Server for AI Coding (Better than Context7)

claim: Crawling documentation through LLM-formatted text files (like llm-text.fold) is faster and more reliable than scraping individual HTML pages recursively
actionable: When crawling documentation, prioritize LLM-structured formats (llm-text.fold, sitemaps) over recursive HTML scraping to reduce latency and improve data quality
source_title: I Built the Ultimate RAG MCP Server for AI Coding (Better than Context7)

claim: Using fixed private knowledge bases reduces hallucinations in AI coding assistants compared to large collective knowledge bases with 8000+ libraries
actionable: Limit RAG knowledge base to only libraries and frameworks actually used in your tech stack to minimize the AI's tendency to reference irrelevant documentation
source_title: I Built the Ultimate RAG MCP Server for AI Coding (Better than Context7)

claim: Implement chunking strategies that preserve document structure (paragraphs and sentences together) rather than arbitrary fixed-size chunks to improve RAG quality
actionable: When implementing chunking logic, use semantic boundaries (paragraphs, code blocks) instead of fixed token counts to maintain context within chunks
source_title: I Built the Ultimate RAG MCP Server for AI Coding (Better than Context7)

claim: You can use local Ollama embeddings instead of OpenAI API for completely private RAG systems, keeping all processing on-device
actionable: Configure embedding models in your RAG MCP server to use Ollama or local alternatives to avoid sending data to external APIs
source_title: I Built the Ultimate RAG MCP Server for AI Coding (Better than Context7)
